items.titleZh: 探索维生素D在家族性地中海热中的作用：发病机制、触发因素和免疫调节
items.abstractZh: 家族性地中海热（FMF）是中东和地中海国家人群中常见的自身炎症性疾病之一。它由MEFV基因突变引起，导致pyrin表达失调，从而引发免疫异常。FMF通过反复发作的发热和浆膜炎症（主要是腹膜炎和胸膜炎）以及其他全身症状来诊断。最近的研究致力于寻找遗传密码之外的因素，这些因素影响FMF的演变、症状严重程度以及对传统治疗药物秋水仙碱的反应。这些因素包括MEFV基因的表观遗传修饰以及其他环境因素，如寒冷暴露、压力、肠道菌群组成和饮食。在这些因素中，维生素D以其在肌肉骨骼健康中的经典作用而闻名，已成为一种强大的免疫调节剂。已有文献记载，维生素D参与促炎细胞因子的调节，并可能调节免疫反应。值得注意的是，在MEFV突变报告患病率最高的一些地区——可能更广泛地反映地中海人群——维生素D浓度通常较低。这种重叠提出了一个假设，即维生素D缺乏可能与FMF发病机制相关，尽管目前的数据主要是相关性，并未建立因果关系。在这篇综述中，我们总结了关于FMF发病机制、潜在触发因素和维生素D代谢的现有证据，并探讨了维生素D如何调节免疫反应以及与关键自身炎症通路的交叉点，同时考虑充足的维生素D补充是否有助于减轻部分FMF患者的疾病负担。

items.title: Treatment Adherence, Psychosocial Factors, and Clinical Outcomes in Repeatedly Hospitalized Patients with Rheumatoid Arthritis and Ankylosing Spondylitis: An Exploratory Mid-Term Longitudinal Mixed-Effects Study.
items.abstract: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and ankylosing spondylitis (AS) are chronic inflammatory rheumatic diseases associated with impaired quality of life, persistent disease burden, and increased healthcare utilization. Treatment adherence and psychosocial factors may influence outcomes, but their longitudinal associations in real-world hospitalized populations remain insufficiently characterized. We conducted a single-center, retrospective, mid-term longitudinal observational study including 50 adults with RA or AS who experienced repeated hospitalizations over a four-year period. The final dataset comprised 196 hospitalization episodes analyzed as repeated observations nested within individual patients. Disease activity was assessed using DAS28 in RA and ASDAS and/or BASDAI in AS, according to data availability and routine clinical practice. Treatment adherence, quality of life, anxiety, social isolation, patient-provider communication, dietary support, inflammatory markers, and hospitalization-related outcomes were extracted from medical records and structured inpatient assessments. Linear mixed-effects models were used for continuous outcomes, and ordinal mixed-effects models were used for ordered categorical outcomes, with adjustment for age, sex, and time where appropriate. In RA, higher treatment adherence was associated with lower disease activity over time. In AS, comparable associations were not detected, possibly reflecting disease-specific factors, limited variability in adherence, and reduced statistical power in the smaller AS subgroup. Better patient-provider communication was associated with higher adherence and lower anxiety, whereas greater social isolation was associated with poorer quality of life. More favorable dietary support was associated with better adherence, although the magnitude of this association should be interpreted cautiously because of sparse categories and wide confidence intervals. Lower inflammatory burden, particularly lower CRP over time, was associated with lower hospitalization-related costs. In this selected cohort of repeatedly hospitalized patients with RA or AS, treatment adherence, psychosocial factors, and supportive care indicators were associated with clinically relevant longitudinal outcomes. The findings support a multidisciplinary, patient-centered approach to inflammatory rheumatic disease care. However, because of the retrospective design, modest sample size, selected inpatient population, non-standardized assessment of several variables, and possible instability of some ordinal model estimates, the results should be interpreted as exploratory and confirmed in larger prospective cohorts.

items.titleZh: 类风湿关节炎和强直性脊柱炎反复住院患者的治疗依从性、心理社会因素与临床结局：一项探索性中期纵向混合效应研究
items.abstractZh: 类风湿关节炎（RA）和强直性脊柱炎（AS）是慢性炎症性风湿病，与生活质量受损、持续疾病负担和医疗资源利用增加相关。治疗依从性和心理社会因素可能影响结局，但它们在真实世界住院人群中的纵向关联尚未得到充分描述。我们进行了一项单中心、回顾性、中期纵向观察性研究，纳入50名在四年期间经历反复住院的RA或AS成人患者。最终数据集包括196次住院事件，作为嵌套在个体患者内的重复观察进行分析。根据数据可用性和常规临床实践，RA患者使用DAS28评估疾病活动度，AS患者使用ASDAS和/或BASDAI评估。从医疗记录和结构化住院评估中提取治疗依从性、生活质量、焦虑、社会隔离、医患沟通、饮食支持、炎症标志物和住院相关结局。连续结局使用线性混合效应模型，有序分类结局使用有序混合效应模型，并酌情调整年龄、性别和时间。在RA中，较高的治疗依从性与随时间较低的疾病活动度相关。在AS中，未检测到类似的关联，可能反映了疾病特异性因素、依从性变异性有限以及较小的AS亚组统计效力降低。更好的医患沟通与较高的依从性和较低的焦虑相关，而更大的社会隔离与较差的生活质量相关。更有利的饮食支持与更好的依从性相关，但由于类别稀疏和置信区间宽，该关联的幅度应谨慎解释。较低的炎症负担，特别是随时间较低的CRP，与较低的住院相关费用相关。在这个反复住院的RA或AS患者队列中，治疗依从性、心理社会因素和支持性护理指标与临床相关的纵向结局相关。研究结果支持对炎症性风湿病采取多学科、以患者为中心的方法。然而，由于回顾性设计、样本量适中、住院人群选择、多个变量的非标准化评估以及某些有序模型估计可能不稳定，结果应视为探索性，并在更大的前瞻性队列中确认。

items.title: Glucagon-like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonists in Rheumatoid Arthritis: A Scoping Review of Metabolic, Anti-Inflammatory, and Cardioprotective Effects.
items.abstract: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a chronic inflammatory disorder associated with a substantially increased risk of cardiovascular (CV) disease, driven by both persistent systemic inflammation and a high burden of traditional cardiometabolic risk factors. In recent years, glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RAs), licensed for type 2 diabetes mellitus and obesity, have attracted attention for their broader metabolic and cardiovascular benefits, raising the question of their potential role in RA. This scoping review summarizes current evidence on the impact of GLP-1RAs on RA disease activity, CV comorbidities, and the underlying immuno-metabolic mechanisms. Experimental studies suggest that GLP-1RAs could modulate key inflammatory pathways in synovial cells, reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine production, oxidative stress, and tissue-degrading enzymes, while improving mitochondrial function. Although clinical data remains limited, observational studies report improvements in disease activity, inflammatory markers, and pain in patients with RA treated with GLP-1RAs in addition to immunosuppressive treatment. Extensive evidence from randomized trials in metabolic populations demonstrates that GLP-1RAs improve glycemic control, induce significant weight loss, and reduce modestly but consistently blood pressure and atherogenic lipids, ultimately lowering major CV events and mortality. Although this evidence cannot be directly translated to RA populations, early real-world data specific to the disease suggest similar favorable trends, including reductions in cardiometabolic risk factors and thromboembolic events. Taken together, these findings suggest that GLP-1RAs may offer dual benefits in RA by addressing both metabolic dysfunction and inflammation. However, the current evidence base is heterogeneous and largely non-randomized, underscoring the need for dedicated trials.

items.titleZh: 胰高血糖素样肽-1受体激动剂在类风湿关节炎中的应用：代谢、抗炎和心脏保护作用的范围综述
items.abstractZh: 类风湿关节炎（RA）是一种慢性炎症性疾病，与心血管（CV）疾病风险显著增加相关，其驱动因素包括持续的全身性炎症和传统心脏代谢危险因素的高负担。近年来，获批用于2型糖尿病和肥胖症的胰高血糖素样肽-1受体激动剂（GLP-1RAs）因其更广泛的代谢和心血管益处而受到关注，引发了其在RA中潜在作用的疑问。本范围综述总结了关于GLP-1RAs对RA疾病活动度、CV合并症以及潜在免疫代谢机制影响的现有证据。实验研究表明，GLP-1RAs可调节滑膜细胞中的关键炎症通路，减少促炎细胞因子产生、氧化应激和组织降解酶，同时改善线粒体功能。尽管临床数据仍然有限，但观察性研究报告称，在接受免疫抑制治疗的基础上加用GLP-1RAs的RA患者中，疾病活动度、炎症标志物和疼痛有所改善。来自代谢人群随机试验的大量证据表明，GLP-1RAs可改善血糖控制，诱导显著体重减轻，并适度但持续地降低血压和致动脉粥样硬化血脂，最终降低主要CV事件和死亡率。尽管这些证据不能直接推广到RA人群，但针对该疾病的早期真实世界数据提示了类似的有利趋势，包括心脏代谢危险因素和血栓栓塞事件的减少。综合来看，这些发现表明GLP-1RAs可能通过同时解决代谢功能障碍和炎症，在RA中提供双重益处。然而，当前证据基础异质性较大且大多为非随机化，强调了进行专门试验的必要性。

items.title: Growth Outcomes in Children with Familial Mediterranean Fever: A Question Beyond Chronic or Relapsing Inflammation.
items.abstract: Background/Objective: Familial Mediterranean fever (FMF) is an autoinflammatory disease caused by missense MEFV mutations, leading to recurrent episodes of interleukin (IL)-1β-mediated inflammation, and represents a model of cytokine-induced growth hormone (GH) resistance. Chronic or relapsing inflammatory bouts may impair growth in FMF children through functional alterations of the GH-insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF-1) axis; however, the impact and reversibility of growth deficit remain unclear. The aim of this review is to assess data related to linear growth in young patients with FMF. Methods: This scoping review was conducted following PRISMA guidelines, searching for studies evaluating growth outcomes in FMF via the PubMed database. Fourteen studies, including 1144 children, were analyzed, evaluating height, growth velocity, IGF-1 levels, and treatment effects of colchicine or IL-1-targeted biologics. Results: Growth was generally preserved in a considerable number of children with FMF. Longitudinal analyses showed improvement in height standard deviation scores (HSDS) along with earlier and higher cumulative doses of colchicine. FMF attack frequency and overall disease severity modestly seemed to influence growth, whereas inflammatory markers were inconsistently correlated with growth parameters. Biologic therapies targeting IL-1 (canakinumab and anakinra) also showed positive effects on HSDS. Children with specific MEFV variants (such as M694V) or higher disease activity scores were at risk of developing a subtle growth impairment. Conclusions: Data on final height, though limited, suggest the preservation of growth in most pediatric patients with FMF. The maintenance of a normal linear growth is related to regular treatment with colchicine, though IL-1 blockers also appear to be beneficial in refractory FMF cases. These data highlight the importance of periodic, proactive check-ups and regular growth monitoring in children with FMF.
